briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to actually be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — The collapse of banking middleware firm Synapse has left thousands of Americans unable to access their savings, raising urgent questions about consumer protections in fintech.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling growing big-tech interest in leveraging open banking data for financial services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

- **[US holds interest rates as Iran war triggers inflation fears](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c36364d06x3o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — The US Federal Reserve is holding interest rates steady as the ongoing Iran conflict stokes inflation concerns, despite presidential pressure to cut.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Bank of Canada holds key interest rate at 2.25%, saying war will boost global inflation](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bank-of-canada-interest-rate-9.7132984?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The Bank of Canada has kept its benchmark rate at 2.25%, citing the Iran conflict as a driver of rising global inflation that warrants caution.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's federal government signalled a move away from screen scraping toward a formal open banking framework, a regulatory shift still shaping the fintech landscape today. *(Note: foundational article relevant to Canada's ongoing open banking implementation.)*

- **[1Password launches new platform to rein in companies' AI agents](https://betakit.com/1password-launches-new-platform-to-rein-in-companies-ai-agents/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian security company 1Password has launched a new platform designed to help businesses manage and govern the growing number of AI agents operating within their systems.

- **[Tailscale makes first acquisition with Border0 purchase](https://betakit.com/tailscale-makes-first-acquisition-with-border0-purchase/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian networking startup Tailscale has made its first-ever acquisition, purchasing Border0 to bolster its secure access capabilities amid rising enterprise demand.

- **[Diesel prices surge to highest since 2022, raising concern for shipping, consumer costs](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/calgary/bakx-diesel-iran-kalibrate-9.7132148?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canadian diesel prices have hit their highest point since 2022 due to Middle East tensions, with knock-on effects expected for shipping costs and everyday consumers. *(Relevant to fintech: embedded finance and payment solutions for logistics and supply chain SMEs face added pressure.)*

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[How Ontario's innovation hubs scale startups across the province](https://betakit.com/how-ontarios-innovation-hubs-scale-startups-across-the-province/)** — *BetaKit* — A look at how Ontario's innovation hubs are supporting startup growth offers a timely model for Atlantic Canada's own ecosystem-building efforts as the region works to scale its fintech and tech sectors.

- **[Canada's population shrank last year — a first for the country, StatsCan says](https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/canada-population-decline-first-time-9.7133643?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's first-ever population decline has significant implications for Atlantic Canada, where financial inclusion strategies and credit union outreach may need to adapt to shrinking and aging communities.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is transforming traditional credit card products with new features and models — a trend Atlantic Canadian credit unions and startups can leverage to differentiate their consumer offerings.
